title: Experiential Learning and Mentoring Programme (ELMP)
text:
Experiential Learning and Mentoring Programme (ELMP) - SupportGoWhere
Experiential Learning and Mentoring Programme (ELMP)
Share link
Supports youths through mentoring and, experiential learning. It provides youths with a safe and conducive place to hang out after school and empowers them with pro-social skills and resilience.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Free
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

The programme engages youth through:

Centre-based drop-in activities like tchoukball training and small group tuitions

Experiential learning activities and workshops

1 to 1 mentoring sessions with mentors

The activities are conducted once a week.

Who is this for?

Youths aged 11 to 19 years old who are enrolled in school social work programmes, as well as those referred from school counsellors and parents.

What to expect?

An intake assessment will be conducted.

Youths will be matched to a suitable mentor thereafter.

How to apply?

Youths or their parents can approach or call the centre directly.

Information last updated on 17 Sep 2025
